Home / Lore / Factions / Rikers

faction

Rikers

A faction originating with escaped Rikers Island prisoners and later recruits, reorganized into splinter groups after the first game.

Connectionnot applicable Continuitydivision game universe Canon scopegame canon Verificationsource reviewed Reviewed2026-07-19

From prison break to faction

The Rikers' identity begins with escaped prisoners and expands as outsiders adopt the group's convict theme. [1]

Lower Manhattan splinter

The faction seen in Warlords of New York is one reorganized splinter rather than an unchanged continuation of the original leadership. [1][2]

Claim ledger

Every factual statement below must retain at least one traceable source.

  1. confirmed

    The Rikers began with escaped prisoners from Rikers Island and later added recruits adopting the faction's convict identity. [1]

  2. confirmed

    After Larae Barret's death, the Rikers became leaderless and split into multiple sub-groups. [1][2]

  3. confirmed

    The Lower Manhattan sub-group is led by James Dragov, one of Aaron Keener's rogue lieutenants. [1]

Related records

  • Strategic Homeland Divisionopposed to
  • Lower Manhattanlocated at

Sources

  1. [1] The Division 2 – State of the Game Recap: Meet the Factions of Warlords of New York — Ubisoft News (2020-02-19). Accessed 2026-07-19.
  2. [2] The Division 2 – State of the Game Recap: The Story So Far — Ubisoft News (2020-02-05). Accessed 2026-07-19.
Editorial status: Later Riker leadership and seasonal activity require separate version tracking.
